terms.definition: Insertion of a sterile tube into the bladder via the urethra to drain urine, using aseptic non-touch technique.
terms.exampleSentence: Please prepare the trolley for urinary catheterisation using a 14Ch Foley.
terms.contextNotes: Document indication; remove as soon as clinically appropriate.

terms.definition: A flexible indwelling urinary catheter retained in the bladder by an inflatable balloon at the tip.
terms.exampleSentence: Inflate the Foley catheter balloon with 10 ml of sterile water.
terms.contextNotes: Sized in Charrière (Ch) — 1Ch ≈ 0.33 mm.

terms.definition: A standardised procedure preventing contamination of key parts and key sites during clinical procedures.
terms.exampleSentence: Use aseptic non-touch technique when accessing the central line.
terms.contextNotes: Standard ANTT for routine; surgical ANTT for complex.

terms.definition: Garments and devices worn by staff to prevent transmission of infection, including gloves, aprons, masks and eye protection.
terms.exampleSentence: Don full personal protective equipment before entering the side room.
terms.contextNotes: Donning and doffing order matters to avoid self-contamination.

terms.definition: A filtering facepiece respirator providing high-level protection against airborne particles, used for aerosol-generating procedures.
terms.exampleSentence: Wear an FFP3 mask before performing tracheal suctioning on this patient.
terms.contextNotes: Requires individual fit-testing per HSE guidance.

terms.definition: The practice of cleaning hands with soap and water or alcohol gel to reduce transmission of micro-organisms.
terms.exampleSentence: Perform hand hygiene before and after every patient contact.
terms.contextNotes: WHO 5 moments framework adopted by NHS.

terms.definition: An NHS dress policy requiring short sleeves and no wrist jewellery to enable effective hand hygiene.
terms.exampleSentence: Please remove your watch — we are bare below the elbows on this ward.
terms.contextNotes: Plain wedding band usually permitted.

terms.definition: A sterile covering applied to a wound to absorb exudate, protect from contamination and promote healing.
terms.exampleSentence: Change the wound dressing daily until exudate reduces.
terms.contextNotes: Choose dressing based on wound bed and exudate level.

terms.definition: An occlusive dressing forming a gel on contact with exudate, suitable for low-to-moderate exudating wounds.
terms.exampleSentence: Apply a hydrocolloid dressing to the category 2 pressure ulcer over the heel.
terms.contextNotes: Brand examples: DuoDERM, Granuflex, Comfeel.

terms.definition: A highly absorbent seaweed-derived dressing for moderate-to-heavy exudating or bleeding wounds.
terms.exampleSentence: Pack the cavity with an alginate dressing and cover with foam.
terms.contextNotes: Brand examples: Kaltostat, Sorbsan.

terms.definition: Administration of medication directly into a muscle, commonly the deltoid, vastus lateralis or ventrogluteal site.
terms.exampleSentence: Give 1 g flucloxacillin as an intramuscular injection into the ventrogluteal site.
terms.contextNotes: Z-track technique reduces leakage and irritation.

terms.definition: Administration of medication into the fatty tissue beneath the skin, typically the abdomen or outer thigh.
terms.exampleSentence: Administer 40 mg enoxaparin as a subcutaneous injection into the abdomen.
terms.contextNotes: Pinch the skin; do not aspirate for low-molecular-weight heparin.

terms.definition: Insertion of a peripheral cannula into a vein to allow IV fluid, medication or blood product administration.
terms.exampleSentence: Please attempt intravenous cannulation in the antecubital fossa with a green cannula.
terms.contextNotes: Document gauge, site and VIP score on insertion.

terms.definition: Visual Infusion Phlebitis score (0-5) used to monitor a peripheral cannula site for inflammation or infection.
terms.exampleSentence: The VIP score is 3 with redness and pain — please re-site the cannula.
terms.contextNotes: Re-site cannula if VIP ≥2.

terms.definition: Leakage of intravenous fluid or medication, particularly vesicants, into surrounding tissue causing damage.
terms.exampleSentence: Stop the infusion immediately — there is suspected extravasation around the cannula.
terms.contextNotes: Aspirate, stop infusion, escalate per trust extravasation policy.

terms.definition: A flexible tube passed through the nose into the stomach for feeding, drug administration or gastric drainage.
terms.exampleSentence: Confirm nasogastric tube position with a pH ≤5.5 before feeding.
terms.contextNotes: Misplacement is a never event — always check pH or X-ray.

terms.definition: Delivery of nutrition directly into the gastrointestinal tract via NG, NJ, PEG or jejunostomy tube.
terms.exampleSentence: Commence enteral feeding at 30 ml/hour and increase as tolerated.
terms.contextNotes: Prescribed by a dietitian; refeeding risk in malnourished patients.

terms.definition: Removal of secretions from the trachea via a suction catheter passed through an endotracheal or tracheostomy tube.
terms.exampleSentence: Pre-oxygenate before tracheal suctioning to minimise desaturation.
terms.contextNotes: Aerosol-generating procedure — wear FFP3.

terms.definition: Therapeutic administration of supplemental oxygen via mask or nasal cannula to maintain target saturations.
terms.exampleSentence: Commence oxygen therapy at 2 L/min via nasal cannulae aiming for sats 94-98%.
terms.contextNotes: Oxygen is a prescribed drug in the BNF.

terms.definition: A fixed-performance oxygen mask delivering a precise oxygen concentration via colour-coded valves.
terms.exampleSentence: Switch to a 28% Venturi mask in this COPD patient to avoid hypercapnia.
terms.contextNotes: Used when controlled FiO2 is essential, e.g. COPD.

terms.definition: A high-flow oxygen mask with a reservoir bag and one-way valves delivering up to 85% oxygen.
terms.exampleSentence: Apply a non-rebreathe mask at 15 L/min while preparing for transfer.
terms.contextNotes: Always inflate the reservoir bag before applying.

terms.definition: Safe placement of needles, blades and other sharp clinical waste into a regulated rigid sharps container.
terms.exampleSentence: Dispose of the needle directly into the sharps bin at the point of use — do not re-sheath.
terms.contextNotes: Never re-sheath needles per HSE guidance.

terms.definition: A percutaneous injury from a contaminated needle or sharp, carrying risk of bloodborne virus transmission.
terms.exampleSentence: Encourage bleeding, wash with soap and water, and report the needlestick injury to occupational health.
terms.contextNotes: Source patient consent needed for blood-borne virus testing.

terms.definition: A nasal and/or groin swab taken on admission to identify meticillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus colonisation.
terms.exampleSentence: Please take an MRSA screening swab from each nostril and the groin.
terms.contextNotes: Positive carriers receive mupirocin nasal ointment + chlorhexidine wash.

terms.definition: Patient placement and PPE practices used to prevent transmission of communicable infections within healthcare settings.
terms.exampleSentence: Place him in a side room under contact isolation precautions for suspected C. difficile.
terms.contextNotes: Source isolation protects others; protective isolation protects the patient.

terms.definition: A spore-forming anaerobic bacterium causing antibiotic-associated colitis, requiring soap-and-water hand hygiene and side-room isolation.
terms.exampleSentence: Send a stool sample for Clostridioides difficile toxin given his recent ciprofloxacin course.
terms.contextNotes: Alcohol gel does not kill spores — wash hands with soap and water.

terms.definition: Pre-administration check: right patient, right drug, right dose, right route, right time.
terms.exampleSentence: Always confirm the five rights of medication before any drug administration.
terms.contextNotes: Some trusts add right documentation and right reason.

terms.definition: A medicine subject to strict legal controls under the Misuse of Drugs Act, requiring two-nurse signing and CD register entry.
terms.exampleSentence: Two registered nurses must witness the controlled drug administration and sign the CD register.
terms.contextNotes: Schedule 2 drugs must be balance-checked at each handover.

terms.definition: A single-dose syringe supplied ready-loaded with medication, reducing preparation errors and contamination risk.
terms.exampleSentence: Administer the enoxaparin pre-filled syringe into the lateral abdominal wall.
terms.contextNotes: Do not expel the air bubble in low-molecular-weight heparin pre-filled syringes.

terms.definition: Administration of donor blood components to a patient, requiring two-person bedside checks and 15-minute observation set.
terms.exampleSentence: Commence the blood transfusion and record observations at 15 minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Each unit must be completed within 4 hours of leaving the fridge.
